COUNSEL FOR GITHUB IS REQUIRED TO MODIFY ITS PROPOSED ORDERS TO SEAL TO REFLECT THE CURRENT PROCEDURE.(THE CURRENT PROCEDURE IS THE COURT SEALS THE ENTIRETY OF A DOCUMENT AND, WHEN THERE ARE PORTIONS OF A DOCUMENT THAT ARE NOT COVERED BY A SEALING ORDER, THE PARTY FILING THE DOCUMENT IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT A REDACTED VERSION OF THE DOCUMENT REDACTING ONLY THE PORTIONS THAT ARE COVERED BY A SEALING ORDER.) COUNSEL FOR GITHUB IS REQUIRED TO MODIFY ITS PROPOSED ORDERS TO SEAL TO REFLECT THE CURRENT PROCEDURE.(THE CURRENT PROCEDURE IS THE COURT SEALS THE ENTIRETY OF A DOCUMENT AND, WHEN THERE ARE PORTIONS OF A DOCUMENT THAT ARE NOT COVERED BY A SEALING ORDER, THE PARTY FILING THE DOCUMENT IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT A REDACTED VERSION OF THE DOCUMENT REDACTING ONLY THE PORTIONS THAT ARE COVERED BY A SEALING ORDER.) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Reply In Support Of Motion For Summary Judgment And Or Summary Adjudication And Supporting Documents
Off Calendar Dec-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GITHUB, INC.'s Motion To Seal Portions Of Reply In Support Of Motion For Summary Judgment And Or Summary Adjudication And Supporting Documents.

Defendant Github, Inc.'s motion to seal portions of Github, Inc.'s reply in support of motion for summary judgment and/or summary adjudication and supporting documents is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown.

Counsel for Github is required to modify its proposed orders to seal to reflect the current procedure.(The current procedure is the court seals the entirety of a document and, when there are portions of a document that are not covered by a sealing order, the party filing the document is required to submit a redacted version of the document redacting only the portions that are covered by a sealing order.)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Allow Plaintiff To File A First Amended Complaint For Violation Of Equal Pay Act And Retaliation
Off Calendar Apr-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Adron W. Beene, Esq., Law Offices of Adron W. Beene, 408-392-9233, appearing for Plaintiff;
Carolina Garcia, Esq & Julia Collins reichert, Esq of Orrick Herrington & sutcliffe LLP, 650-289-7163, appearing for Defendant Gitthub, Inc.;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANGES PAK'S Motion For Order To Allow Plaintiff To File A First Amended Complaint For Violation Of Equal Pay Act And Retaliation.

Plaintiff Agnes Pak's motion for order to allow Plaintiff to file a first amended complaint for violation of Equal Pay Act and retaliation is granted. The liberal policy of amendment supports the motion. Plaintiff need not prove the corrected allegations she seeks to make, but need only specify those matters listed in Rule 3.1324, including when the facts giving rise to the amendment were discovered. Defendant Github, Inc. has not shown that the minor changes Plaintiff seeks to make in her complaint fall within the sham pleading doctrine. "Critically, the sham pleading doctrine 'cannot be mechanically applied.'" (JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. v. Ward (Mar. 28, 2019) 2019 WL 1396740 at *8.) In particular, the doctrine "was not intended to preclude plaintiffs from providing additional and noncontradictory allegations." (Leasequip, Inc. v. Dapeer (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 394, 405 n.6; see also Zakk v. Diesel (Mar. 25, 2019) 2019 WL 1349530 at *11-12 [allegations regarding contract claims in third amended complaint "merely clarifies the basis for [plaintiff's] claims for relief" and sham pleading doctrine therefore did not apply].) Nor does Defendant show that it will be prejudiced if the amendment is allowed.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
